reason: PROP-016 Mech A: EXP-294 patches target build-scripts/generate-html.js (NEVER_PUSH infrastructure file). Cannot be self-applied by decider. Stranded for operator manual application in own-clone-with-direct-push.
related_issues_unaffected: ISS-1087 (does not acknowledge methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat)
ISS-1219 (cross-page anchor stability for Timestamp Error subsection IDs)

build-scripts/generate-html.js.description: Three find/replace patches from EXP-294 to harmonize Timestamp Error tab with already-harmonized WIN-035-039/part1/part6/part8 prose

patches.target_path: Timestamp Error tab — 'What gets timestamped' scorecard card (~line 1822)
patches.uniqueness_verified: Substring count = 1 in current build-scripts/generate-html.js (verified 2026-05-08 against origin/main HEAD).
patches.find: status_history.json — observed values, pass/fail audit results, statistical comparisons. This is the answer sheet.
patches.replace: status_history.json — bundles prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance) together with observation-side fields (observed value, pass/fail, error percentage) per domain per commit. The bundle is the answer sheet plus the question key on the same page; the OTS hash covers both — but cannot show one preceded the other.

patches.target_path: Timestamp Error tab — main body paragraph immediately under the scorecard (~line 1832)
patches.find: OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, the file containing reference data: observed values, pass/fail results, and statistical comparisons. These are observations — the outcomes. The prediction parameters — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html, which are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.
patches.replace: OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, a file whose per-domain entries bundle prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail) at every commit. Both sides land in the file simultaneously — the OTS hash covers the bundle, not a temporal ordering between the prediction text and the observation it is paired with. The structural prediction logic — the code that computes the predicted field — lives in monitor.py source code, with the static prediction text mirrored in docs/model.html; both are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.

patches.target_path: Timestamp Error tab — 'Which Claims This Affects' WIN-035 through WIN-039 list item (~line 1888)
patches.find: • WIN-035 through WIN-039 — described by the dome as prospective, with predictions registered via OpenTimestamps before data arrival. These are the WINs where the OTS chain is the primary evidence. But the OTS chain timestamps status_history.json (observations), not the prediction parameters.
patches.replace: • WIN-035 through WIN-039 — described by the dome as prospective, with predictions registered via OpenTimestamps before data arrival. These are the WINs where the OTS chain is the primary evidence. But the OTS chain timestamps status_history.json — a file whose per-domain entries bundle prediction-side fields (formula, predicted, tolerance) and observation-side fields (observed, pass/fail) at every commit — and not an isolated predictions-only document timestamped before data arrival.

operator_action: 1) cd to your clone with direct push. 2) For each patch in per_file_diffs["build-scripts/generate-html.js"].patches, find the exact string in build-scripts/generate-html.js and replace with the replacement text. 3) git add build-scripts/generate-html.js. 4) Run: node build.js html && tests/run.sh. 5) git commit -m "EXP-294: harmonize Timestamp Error tab with OTS WIN prose" && git push. 6) Verify grep: grep -lE "These are observations — the outcomes|observed values, pass/fail audit results, statistical comparisons. This is the" build-scripts/generate-html.js data/sections.json data/wins.json should return zero matches. 7) Close ISS-1875 after confirming zero matches. 8) Move this file to monitor/decisions/applied-stranded-patches/
post_apply_verification: After the decider applies EXP-294 P1/P2/P3 to build-scripts/generate-html.js, run `node build.js html` and `tests/run.sh` (per CLAUDE.md 'Run Tests Before Committing Data Changes' rule, since this touches a docs/index.html-affecting file). The HTML build will surface any malformed substitutions immediately. Then verify cross-page consistency with a single grep: `grep -lE 'These are observations — the outcomes|observed values, pass/fail audit results, statistical comparisons. This is the' build-scripts/generate-html.js data/sections.json data/wins.json` should return zero matches.
closure_note: After patches verified in main: close ISS-1875 with status:fixed, fixed_by:operator-applied-stranded. Mark EXP-294 as integrated:true in expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl.
